Title: Jasmonate promotes artemisinin biosynthesis by activating the TCP14-ORA complex in Artemisia annua

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: artemisinic aldehyde Δ11(13) reductase / double bond reductase 2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C11(13) double bond of artemisinic aldehyde to form dihydroartemisinic aldehyde, directing flux toward the dihydroartemisinic acid/artemisinin branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor branch toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated in leaves/trichomes; induced in lines overexpressing AaTCP14, AaORA, or AaTCP14-AaORA; repressed in AaTCP14 antisense, AaJAZ8 overexpression, and AaTCP14 antisense-AaORA lines relative to AaORA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate (MeJA) treatment enhances promoter activation through AaTCP14/AaORA complex
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DBR2 promoter in Artemisia annua; 35S promoter for transgene expression in assays; MET25 inducible promoter used for bridge expression in Y3H context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DBR2 expression promotes artemisinin accumulation; DBR2 is a key enzyme activated by the AaTCP14-AaORA complex and derepressed upon JA-triggered AaJAZ8 degradation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC promoter activation assays; qRT-PCR in transgenic plants; Y1H; EMSA; metabolite HPLC in transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; essential for artemisinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBR2 is a major direct target of AaTCP14 and AaORA. 2. AaTCP14 binds a TCP-binding site in the DBR2 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. AaORA synergizes with AaTCP14 to further enhance DBR2 expression, while AaJAZ8 represses this activation until JA promotes AaJAZ8 degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for increasing artemisinin when combined with positive regulators such as AaTCP14 and AaORA or by relieving JAZ repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activation is stronger with MeJA; repression by AaJAZ8 is partly through disruption of the AaTCP14-AaORA complex rather than DNA-binding inhibition of AaTCP14.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes dihydroartemisinic aldehyde to dihydroartemisinic acid in the artemisinin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated in AaTCP14 overexpression, AaORA overexpression, and AaTCP14-AaORA co-overexpression plants; reduced in AaTCP14 antisense, AaJAZ8 overexpression, and AaTCP14 antisense-AaORA plants relative to AaORA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA enhances promoter activation by AaTCP14 and AaORA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ALDH1 promoter in Artemisia annua; 35S promoter for transgene expression in assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ALDH1 expression is associated with increased artemisinin biosynthesis, especially when AaTCP14 and AaORA are coexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC promoter activation assays; qRT-PCR in transgenic plants; Y1H; EMSA; metabolite HPLC in transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late-pathway step, but rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALDH1 is a direct transcriptional target of AaTCP14. 2. AaORA cooperates with AaTCP14 to enhance ALDH1 promoter activity and transcript abundance. 3. Reduced AaTCP14 weakens AaORA-mediated activation of ALDH1, linking ALDH1 control to the TCP14-ORA complex.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful late-pathway engineering target together with DBR2 and the positive regulators AaTCP14/AaORA for artemisinin improvement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaTCP14 binds the A1 TCP-binding motif in the ALDH1 promoter; unlike DBR2, repression by AaJAZ8 is mechanistically inferred through inhibition of the regulator complex.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I TCP transcription factor; TCP domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DBR2 and ALDH1 directly; positively associated with higher ADS and CYP71AV1 transcript levels indirectly; up-regulates AaORA, AaMYC2, AaWRKY1, AaGSW1, AaAOC, and AaOPR3 in transgenic plants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; cooperative complex formation with AaORA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible positive feedback with JA biosynthesis/signaling inferred from activation of AaAOC and AaOPR3 and JA-responsive expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms transcriptional activator complex with AaORA; interaction is antagonized by AaJAZ8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves and trichomes; induced by MeJA and peaks at 9 h; also expressed in stems, leaves, shoots, flowers, buds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young leaves and trichomes; MeJA inducible
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM MeJA induces expression and strengthens promoter activation; no obvious morphological differences in overexpression or antisense plants under normal conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient assays; native promoter analyzed by proTCP14:GUS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator and JA-responsive activator that directly controls key late-pathway genes and cooperates with AaORA to boost artemisinin production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases artemisinin and DHAA and decreases AA; antisense suppression reduces artemisinin, DHAA, and AA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and antisense transgenics; HPLC metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pulldown; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaTCP14 is a trichome-enriched, JA-responsive nuclear TCP factor that directly binds the DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters. 2. AaTCP14 positively regulates artemisinin biosynthesis, as overexpression raises artemisinin while antisense knockdown lowers it. 3. AaTCP14 forms a synergistic activator complex with AaORA, and this complex is repressed by AaJAZ8.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for artemisinin enhancement, especially in combination with AaORA or strategies that reduce JAZ repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological penalty reported in overexpression or antisense lines under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaORA does not change AaTCP14 DNA-binding ability in vitro; AaTCP14 C-terminus mediates interaction with both AaORA and AaJAZ8.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ADS, CYP71AV1, DBR2, and ALDH1 promoters; synergistically enhances DBR2 and ALDH1 activation together with AaTCP14
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via protein complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Methyl jasmonate signaling; likely downstream of AaMYC2/AaGSW1 pathway as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA-responsive feedforward network for artemisinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms activator complex with AaTCP14; also interacts with AaJAZ8 which disrupts complex function
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in trichomes/young leaves; induced by MeJA and peaks at 9 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young leaves and trichomes; MeJA inducible
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA enhances its promoter activation function and relieves AaJAZ8-mediated repression via proteasomal degradation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient assays and overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive JA-responsive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis whose activation of late-pathway genes is substantially strengthened by AaTCP14
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases DBR2/ALDH1 expression and artemisinin; its positive effect is reduced when AaTCP14 is silenced
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC; overexpression and combinatorial transgenics; qRT-PCR; HPLC; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pulldown; protoplast transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaORA positively regulates artemisinin biosynthesis and physically interacts with AaTCP14. 2. AaORA and AaTCP14 synergistically activate DBR2 and ALDH1, increasing artemisinin accumulation. 3. AaORA function on these targets is partly dependent on AaTCP14 and is antagonized by AaJAZ8.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for stacking with AaTCP14 to increase artemisinin production
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological penalty reported for co-overexpression with AaTCP14 under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaORA alone activates ADS and CYP71AV1 promoters, indicating both AaTCP14-dependent and AaTCP14-independent regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ protein with ZIM and Jas domains
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; nuclear bodies when associated with partners
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AaTCP14-AaORA complex; DBR2 promoter activation output; also interacts with AaMYC2 as context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses activation of DBR2 by AaTCP14, AaORA, and their complex; lowers ADS, CYP71AV1, DBR2, and ALDH1 transcript levels when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression and competitor/disruptor of activator complex; relieved by ubiquitin/26S proteasome-dependent degradation after JA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Methyl jasmonate/jasmonate perception via COI1; 26S proteasome
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA negative-feedback repression module; MeJA induces AaJAZ8 transcript but also promotes its protein degradation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associates with both AaTCP14 and AaORA and relocates/recruits their complex to nuclear bodies; forms repressive tripartite module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed and highest in trichomes; induced by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA promotes AaJAZ8 degradation; MG132 blocks this degradation; MeJA partially reverses AaJAZ8-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key JA-pathway repressor that directly restrains the decisive AaTCP14-AaORA activator complex until JA triggers its degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression lowers artemisinin and reduces expression of core pathway genes; a Δjas dominant form strengthens repression by preventing JA-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and Δjas transgenics; qRT-PCR; HPLC; Y2H; BiFC; colocalization; Co-IP; LUC complementation; Y3H; dual-LUC; protein degradation assay with MG132/MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaJAZ8 is a negative regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. It directly interacts with both AaTCP14 and AaORA, repressing their transcriptional activation activity and weakening the AaTCP14-AaORA complex. 3. JA promotes AaJAZ8 degradation through the 26S proteasome, thereby releasing activator function and enhancing artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for knockout/editing or degradation tuning to derepress artemisinin biosynthesis, with caution due to broader JA signaling roles
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on JA responses are possible but not directly measured here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only AaJAZ8 among tested AaJAZ proteins interacted with AaTCP14; its ZIM-domain-containing region mediates interaction with AaTCP14 and AaORA.
